text.title: embeddings-production-ops Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for embeddings-production-ops as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Starting production without monitoring for cache hit rate, error rate, and latency — only adding monitoring after cost spikes or user complaints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Embedding cost and quality regressions are invisible without metrics. Cache misconfiguration produces 10x cost overruns silently. Error rate spikes from rate limits corrupt indexing jobs without visible exceptions. Set up monitoring on day one.

failure-mode.description: Partial model migration: re-embedding only new documents with the new model while leaving old documents with the old model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This produces embedding drift — the index contains vectors from two incompatible geometric spaces. Similarity scores become meaningless and recall degrades silently. Full re-embedding is the only safe migration path.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring silent truncation: embedding documents that exceed the token limit without pre-truncation or chunking.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All embedding models silently truncate at their token limit (typically 512-8192 tokens). The embedding reflects only the truncated portion. Long documents must be chunked before embedding — see chunking-strategies methodology.
